purpose: Prove IAMScope can emit a validated positive admin path for a narrow real-AWS assume-role benchmark.
claim_surface: IAMScope can emit validated admin_reachability for a real minimal assume-role path.
IAMScope can avoid falsely blocking or demoting that exact positive benchmark path.
benchmark_proves: For the Env06 alice->admin path, IAMScope emitted at least one validated admin_reachability finding.
For that path, IAMScope emitted zero blocked admin_reachability findings.
For that path, IAMScope emitted zero inconclusive admin_reachability findings.
benchmark_does_not_prove: It does not prove broader positive-path coverage beyond this narrow direct assume-role case.
Phase 0 scoring does not separately machine-check blocker-free validated findings for this case.
unknowns_remaining: How validated admin reachability behaves across longer chains or richer trust-policy shapes remains unproven here.
How stable the same positive-path result is across more complex mixed-signal environments remains unknown here.

environment.notes: Single-account IAM-only direct assume-role positive path.

ground_truth.reason: Alice can directly assume env06-admin and the target role is admin-equivalent.

directly_proven_if_pass: This exact positive admin path is emitted as validated.
This exact positive admin path is not emitted as blocked or inconclusive.
strongly_supported_if_pass: The current positive admin-reachability path is coherent for this narrow real-AWS case.
only_implied_even_if_pass: Broader positive-path coverage outside this exact case remains only implied.
